outcomes.note: Fill in as results arrive on later runs.

correctionsFromAppendixA: Opta refreshed post-QF (25k sims): title FRA 34.0 / ESP 23.4 / ENG 21.9 / ARG 20.6; reach-final FRA 57.7 / ESP 42.3 / ENG 50.9 / ARG 49.1. The '49.1% ARG vs ENG' figure is ARG's reach-final (win the SF) probability.

Silver Bulletin 'PELE' per-team title numbers are paywalled; methodology (Elo/SPI-style, 100k sims, daily) + reputation noted; public prediction-market co-favorite figure (~FRA/ESP 17% pre-QF) cited instead of a fabricated number.
Bookmakers (FanDuel via FOX, Jul 12): FRA +140, ENG +310, ESP +330, ARG +400; Oddschecker aggregate differs in absolute price, same ordering. Implied % includes overround.
Penalty superlative softened: 8 penalties (5 in 2022, 3 in 2026) = most in recent World Cups, ~double any other side; dropped unsourced 'most in tournament history over a 12-match span'.
Cape Verde '~23M views' figure DROPPED — unverifiable.
Xhaka quote is a paraphrase ('a decision where you kill the game'), attributed as such, not a verbatim 'killed the game'.
Grondona labelled DOJ's 'Soccer Official #1' (not 'Co-Conspirator #1'); identified in reporting, deceased 2014, never charged.
TourProdEnter $260M/$57M split attributed to La Nación (single-source); widely-cited figure is '$300M+'. Private individuals referred to by role, not name.
'Two law-enforcement sources' attributed specifically to the Miami Herald.
Prosecutor names (Berger/Gushue/Ting) attributed to La Nación (single-source).
Tapia et al. 'procesados' (processed/charged) Mar 2026 for ~$12.7M social-security evasion, upheld on appeal Jun 2026; travel bond (30M pesos) requires return before Jul 21.
Dec 9 2025 raids hit AFA + 17 clubs (some outlets say 18, overlapping case).
Calciopoli '~150k wiretapped calls' softened/press-attributed; Grimes exponential form confirmed as the constant-population special case of the corrected model.
